concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that almost all of the images extracted from the PDF have a 'valid: false' status due to a processing error ('No module named 'PIL''). This means that the base64 data for these images is corrupt or truncated. Even though tags are present in the HTML, the browser will not be able to render these images. This affects numerous meaningful images, including the cover photo, hook diagrams, and data charts.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Meaningful images such as the cover landscape photo (page 1), hook diagrams (page 70), and MAFMC logo (page 81).
concerns.output_quote: The images are included as tags, but the base64 data is corrupt/invalid according to the image manifest, meaning they will appear as broken images in a browser.

concerns.description: Fidelity Content evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The content fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Fidelity Structural evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The structural fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: In Appendix A (page 203), the source PDF contains a scanned letter from the National Marine Fisheries Service. The HTML output contains both large images that appear to be full or partial renderings of the letter, as well as the fully extracted text of the letter directly alongside it (e.g., 'UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE...'). This causes the user to see the same content duplicated once as an image and once as structured text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Appendix A letter from the National Marine Fisheries Service.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML includes images of the letter (alt="Official letter dated July 19, 2017...") followed by the exact same extracted text content ('UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE...').

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 9 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=9. Examples: pages 125-126: 3 cols (year, date(s), regulation change...); pages 126-127: 3 cols (year, date(s), regulation change...); pages 127-128: 3 cols (year, date(s), regulation change...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 15 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=15. Examples: p57: "January"; p57: "February"; p57: "March". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Data row marked as header: 2 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row
concerns.analysis: data_as_header=2. Examples: p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]; p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]. Header rows should contain column labels, not data values.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 145 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=145, examples=['http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-book', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (255): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=255. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 48 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: <Lara.Klibansky@ncdenr.gov; report.spam@nc.gov; <r.bizzell.mfc@ncdenr.gov; <Tom.Roller.mfc@ncdenr.gov; <t.hendrickson.mfc@ncdenr.gov
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=48. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 9 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=9. Examples: p42: [portal.ncdenr.org; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "***Times indicated are merely for guidance. The co". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 8 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "WHO:", "MIKE BLANTONElizabeth City", "MIKE BLANTONElizabeth City"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=8. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 38 link(s) clustered at bottom of 14 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=14, total_displaced=38. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "may 14, 2020"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "march 18, 2020"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "or 252-808-8022."
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "15a ncac 03m .0509 tarpon"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "2020-2021 rulemaking cycle"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Watermark/stamp text "APPROVED BY" appears on 6/248 pages
concerns.analysis: watermark="APPROVED BY", page_count=6/248. Repeated watermark/stamp text indicates a document overlay that may be duplicated in the HTML output.

concerns.description: Flattened sub-lists: 4 item(s) contain nested sub-items (a/b/c or bullets) as plain text instead of structured children
concerns.analysis: flattened_sublists=4. Examples: p4 table: • Division of Marine Fisheries Quarterly, • Recreational Hook and Line Modificatio, • Informational Materials:; p5 table: • Status of Ongoing Plans – Catherine Bl, • Blue Crab Fishery Management Plan Amen, – Presentation on proposed Diamondback T; p5 table: • 2019/2020 rulemaking cycle, • 2020/2021 rulemaking cycle, • Vote on Notice of Text for readoption . Sub-items embedded as newline-delimited text lose semantic nesting in HTML — screen readers can't navigate the hierarchy.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."; 18 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=18. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 43 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 6: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-b"; page 11: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_fi"; page 11: "e=DLFE-142450.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142457.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142458.pdf"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=43. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
